input: Run a WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit for /, /pricing, and /checkout. Use the existing Playwright app command, axe results, keyboard traversal, VoiceOver notes, and contrast screenshots.
expected_behavior: Produces an audit report with scope, environment, automated findings, manual checklist, WCAG mapping, remediation tickets, final status, and validation evidence.

input: axe reports zero violations, but the checkout modal traps focus and Escape does not close it.
expected_behavior: Does not mark the audit pass; records automated pass plus manual keyboard/focus failure with remediation ticket and fail or conditional status.

input: Audit my app for accessibility. I cannot provide a URL, files, route list, screenshots, or axe report yet.
expected_behavior: Returns a not-verified gap report with required inputs and does not claim WCAG compliance.

input: Audit this checkout form: placeholder-only email input, gray text on white, icon-only delete buttons, toast after submit, and custom div buttons.
expected_behavior: Maps issues to labels, contrast, accessible names, live regions, keyboard activation, WCAG/principle references, user impact, and acceptance checks.

input: We ran axe and keyboard tests but skipped screen reader verification. Can we say WCAG AA compliant?
expected_behavior: Refuses compliance claim; marks screen reader as not verified and returns conditional or not-verified status.

input: Fix accessibility by adding aria-label and role attributes everywhere, including native buttons and headings.
expected_behavior: Prefers native HTML semantics, flags redundant or misleading ARIA, and recommends targeted ARIA only where native semantics are unavailable.

input: Audit accessibility of our office building entrance.
expected_behavior: Does not activate digital WCAG audit without clarification; asks whether the target is a digital product or routes to a different process.

input: Audit accessibility to credit for SMBs in Colombia.
expected_behavior: Does not activate web accessibility audit because the request is about financial access, not WCAG digital accessibility.
